agent.name: synthesizer
agent.role: Merge, deduplicate, normalize, rank, and plan across all agent outputs.

coverage.notes: Merged outputs from audits/runs/2026-04-07. UX agent reported coverage_complete true; others partial.

findings.title: GitHub webhook accepts any signature header without HMAC verification
findings.description: The Supabase Edge function for GitHub webhooks rejects requests in production only when the x-hub-signature-256 header is missing. It never computes HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body with GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET or compares it to the header.

proof_hooks.summary: Production only requires presence of signature header; no HMAC verify implementation before processing body.
Illustrative attack shape against a deployed endpoint (verify legally on your own stack only).
steps: Obtain the public URL of the github-webhook function.
POST JSON mimicking a push event with header x-hub-signature-256: sha256=fake.
Observe that the request is not rejected for bad MAC (only missing header fails in production).

repro_steps: Deploy or run the function with NODE_ENV=production.
POST valid JSON with x-github-event: push and x-hub-signature-256: sha256=00 (non-empty).
Confirm handler proceeds past the signature check without cryptographic verification.
findings.impact: Unauthorized job enqueue into audit_runs, compute spend, and database writes as the Supabase service role.

suggested_fix.approach: Verify GitHub's HMAC-SHA256: read raw body, compute HMAC with GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET, timing-safe compare to x-hub-signature-256 (strip sha256= prefix). Reject on mismatch. Use raw body before JSON parse.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Ensure the handler uses the raw request body for HMAC; parsing JSON first can break signature verification.
tests_needed: Unit test: valid signature passes; wrong secret fails; tampered body fails.
Integration: GitHub delivery with real signature in staging.

history.notes: Merged from security agent output; no prior open_findings state.

findings.title: Worker throws on startup of every audit job: core_system_prompt.md missing at repo audits path
findings.description: loadClusterPrompts resolves repoRoot()/audits/prompts/core_system_prompt.md and throws if neither that nor legacy path exists. Any processJob run fails at prompt load unless layout is restored.

proof_hooks.summary: apps/worker/src/process-job.ts loadClusterPrompts throws when coreResolved missing (lines 99–107).
Glob audits/prompts/**/core_system_prompt.md at repo root returns 0 files; apps/worker/audits/prompts/core_system_prompt.md exists separately.

history.notes: Confirmed throw path and missing file at expected path.
Merged from logic agent output.

findings.title: Root global-error UI has no path back to portfolio (unlike app/error.tsx)
findings.description: Segment-level error.tsx offers Try again plus a Link to /. global-error.tsx only renders a heading, raw message, and Try again with no link to / or full layout. After a root layout failure, users lack the same recovery affordance as nested route errors.

proof_hooks.summary: apps/dashboard/app/global-error.tsx: return body has h1, p with error.message, button reset only — no Link href="/".
apps/dashboard/app/error.tsx:38-49 — Try again plus Link href="/" labeled Portfolio for comparison.
Global root error surface (any fault that triggers global-error boundary).

history.notes: Compared global-error vs segment error components.

findings.title: Product name casing is inconsistent (Penny vs penny)
findings.description: README and repo branding use “Penny”; root metadata title and in-app strings use lowercase “penny” (e.g. layout metadata, document.title suffixes, footer). Decide a single public-facing rule (sentence case in prose, lowercase wordmark in UI, etc.) and align metadata, titles, and marketing copy.

findings.title: Portfolio project card overlay buttons rely on title= only (export/remove)
findings.description: Export (↓) and remove (×) controls use title attributes for tooltips but omit aria-label. Screen reader users get unclear accessible names compared to visible icon-only affordances elsewhere that use aria-label (e.g. dismiss buttons on the same page).

proof_hooks.summary: apps/dashboard/app/page.tsx:610-630 — buttons with title="Export" and title="Remove" only.

history.notes: Compared with other buttons on page using aria-label.

findings.description: Sidebar nav shows “Activity” (Shell.tsx NAV_ITEMS) while the page eyebrow also says Activity but the H1 reads “Job queue”. Not wrong, but users may wonder if they are the same section; consider aligning headline with nav or adding a short subline.

findings.title: Browser tab title only updated on home and project pages
findings.description: document.title is set in app/page.tsx and ProjectPageClient for portfolio and project contexts. /activity and /repairs leave the default metadata title (“penny”), so multi-tab users cannot distinguish Activity vs Repairs from the tab alone.

proof_hooks.summary: apps/dashboard/app/page.tsx:101-103 — useEffect sets document.title for portfolio only.
/activity and /repairs — no matching document.title updates found in JobQueueView or EngineView.

history.notes: grep apps/dashboard for document.title shows only two call sites.
Normalized proof_hooks from agent hooks[] field.

findings.title: Orchestration enqueue secret can be read from sessionStorage by page scripts
findings.description: apiFetchWithEnqueueSecret merges a user-supplied enqueue secret from sessionStorage into Authorization headers. Any XSS in the dashboard origin can read sessionStorage and exfiltrate the secret.

proof_hooks.summary: Session storage value is attached as Bearer token when present.

findings.impact: A successful XSS can steal the shared enqueue secret and replay API calls until rotation.

suggested_fix.approach: Prefer httpOnly cookie-only auth for orchestration; if a second factor is required, use short-lived CSRF-protected forms or subresource isolation. Minimize secret lifetime in sessionStorage.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Changing auth UX may affect operators who paste secrets into the UI.

findings.title: Supabase helpers use select('*') with no row limits
findings.description: Dashboard helpers fetch entire rows for projects, audit_runs, findings, model_usage, webhooks, and schedules. Large JSON columns (metadata, proof_hooks, history) inflate payloads and memory on the server and client. getFindings has no default LIMIT, so projects with many findings pay full-table transfer per call.

proof_hooks.value: .from('findings').select('*').eq('project_id', projectId)
.from('projects').select('*')

suggested_fix.approach: Replace select('*') with explicit column lists for list views; add optional limit/range parameters to getFindings and callers; consider server-side pagination for the dashboard.
steps: Identify columns required by each UI surface.
Add .select('id, title, ...') and .range() or .limit() where appropriate.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.

findings.title: Next.js NFT tracing warns that the whole monorepo may be pulled into the server trace
findings.description: Production build logs report an unexpected file in the NFT list, linking next.config.ts to dynamic filesystem usage (mergeEnvLocal) and API routes importing onboarding helpers. That pattern can enlarge traced server bundles and slow cold starts on Netlify/Vercel-style deploys.

proof_hooks.value: Encountered unexpected file in NFT list — import trace: next.config.ts → lib/onboarding.ts → app/api/projects/[name]/onboarding/cluster/route.ts
mergeEnvLocal reads repo root .env.local at config load

suggested_fix.approach: Scope filesystem reads to build-time only or move env merging to runtime bootstrap; narrow onboarding imports so API routes do not drag unrelated files into the trace.
steps: Review Next 16 docs on outputFileTracingRoot and turbopackIgnore hints from the build log.
Split onboarding cluster logic so the route imports a minimal module.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.

findings.title: next/font Google Fonts requires network at build time
findings.description: layout.tsx uses next/font/google for Inter, JetBrains Mono, and DM Serif Display. The preflight build failed because Google Fonts could not be fetched (sandboxed/offline build). CI and locked-down environments need self-hosted or committed font files to avoid flaky builds and external dependency on fonts.googleapis.com during `next build`.

proof_hooks.value: Failed to fetch Inter / JetBrains Mono / DM Serif Display from Google Fonts
import { DM_Serif_Display, Inter, JetBrains_Mono } from "next/font/google"

suggested_fix.approach: Switch to next/font/local with files in apps/dashboard/public/fonts or use a build-time font pipeline that does not require live HTTPS to Google.
steps: Vendor font files and reference via localFont.
Document network requirements for CI if keeping next/font/google.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.

findings.title: Portfolio audit jobs process projects sequentially
findings.description: After resolveProjectsForJob, the worker loops `for (const project of projects)` and runs executeProjectAudit and LLM work per project. Wall-clock time and queue depth scale linearly with portfolio size; cost scales with the number of projects × audits. Parallelism exists inside passes (PASS_CONCURRENCY) but not across projects in this loop.

proof_hooks.value: for (const project of projects) { ... await executeProjectAudit(project, payload, pool);

suggested_fix.approach: If product allows, enqueue one BullMQ child job per project or cap concurrency with a semaphore to improve throughput without exceeding provider rate limits.
steps: Define max concurrent projects per portfolio job from env.
Measure Redis/Postgres load before raising concurrency.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.

findings.title: Project page loads the full portfolio via /api/projects
findings.description: ProjectPageClient uses usePortfolioProjects, which always GETs /api/projects and filters client-side to the current project. For users with many projects this duplicates work and payload size versus a single-project GET.

proof_hooks.value: useEffect(() => { void fetchProjects(); void fetchQueue(); }, ...)
const res = await apiFetch("/api/projects");

suggested_fix.approach: Add GET /api/projects/[name] as the primary load for the project route (or hydrate from server component) and keep list fetch for the portfolio index only.
steps: Compare existing /api/projects/[name] behavior with ProjectPageClient data needs.
Reduce redundant refetches after navigation between projects.

findings.title: auditWithLlm sets maxTokens to 12288 for every audit call
findings.description: The worker requests up to 12k output tokens per audit call. That raises worst-case cost per call for providers that bill by generated tokens, even when findings JSON is small. Tiered limits by audit_kind or response-size heuristics would reduce tail spend.

proof_hooks.value: maxTokens: 12288

suggested_fix.approach: Lower default maxTokens for narrow agents; keep a higher cap only for synthesis or intelligence passes; validate with token usage metrics from model_usage.
steps: Histogram completion sizes by agent from model_usage.
Set per-audit_kind limits in routing config.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.

findings.title: Should findings have a composite index on (project_id, status, created_at)?
findings.description: Migrations define idx_findings_project and idx_findings_status separately. Dashboard queries often filter by project_id and optionally status with ordering by created_at. A composite index may reduce heap fetches versus BitmapAnd of two indexes, but this should be validated with real row counts and plans.

proof_hooks.value: CREATE INDEX idx_findings_project ON findings(project_id); CREATE INDEX idx_findings_status ON findings(status);
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT id, title FROM findings WHERE project_id = $1 AND status = $2 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 50;

suggested_fix.approach: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on production-like data; add CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY on (project_id, status, created_at DESC) if seq scans or high buffer reads appear.
steps: Capture baseline plan from Supabase SQL editor.
Compare before/after index addition in a staging branch.

findings.title: Repository has no CI/CD pipeline
findings.description: There is no `.github/workflows/` directory and no automated jobs to run lint, typecheck, tests, or build on push/PR. Root `package.json` defines `lint`, `typecheck`, `test`, and `build` via Turbo, but nothing enforces them before merge or deploy.

proof_hooks.value: Glob of `.github/workflows` returns 0 files.

proof_hooks.summary: Scripts exist but unrunnable in CI
proof_hooks.value: pnpm run lint | typecheck | test | build — no matching automation.

suggested_fix.approach: Add a GitHub Actions workflow (or other hosted CI) that installs pnpm, caches store, and runs turbo lint, typecheck, test, and build on pull_request and main.
steps: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm run lint && pnpm run typecheck && pnpm run test && pnpm run build

findings.title: Dashboard ESLint config imports a package not declared in dependencies
findings.description: `eslint.config.mjs` imports `FlatCompat` from `@eslint/eslintrc`, but `apps/dashboard/package.json` does not list `@eslint/eslintrc`. `pnpm run lint` fails with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND, so the lint gate is broken for local dev and would fail in CI once added.

proof_hooks.value: Cannot find package '@eslint/eslintrc' imported from .../eslint.config.mjs
import { FlatCompat } from "@eslint/eslintrc";

suggested_fix.approach: Add `@eslint/eslintrc` (and any peer deps ESLint 9 flat config needs) to `apps/dashboard` devDependencies, or replace FlatCompat with native flat config without @eslint/eslintrc.
steps: pnpm add -D @eslint/eslintrc --filter penny-dashboard
pnpm run lint

That pattern risks oversized serverless traces or incorrect file inclusion for deploy targets that rely on NFT output.

proof_hooks.value: next.config.ts — Encountered unexpected file in NFT list — import trace includes app/api/.../onboarding/cluster/route.ts

suggested_fix.approach: Scope filesystem reads in config and shared modules to a dedicated data directory, gate `mergeEnvLocal` to development-only, or refactor onboarding cluster route to avoid pulling next.config into the server bundle trace.
steps: Review import chain onboarding.ts → next.config
Use static paths or turbopackIgnore comments as suggested by the build output

findings.title: Dashboard penny_* tables have no RLS in migrations
findings.description: Migrations create penny_projects, penny_audit_jobs, penny_audit_runs, penny_repair_jobs, penny_maintenance_*, penny_project_manifests, penny_linear_sync, etc., without ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY or policies. The auth-scoped LYRA tables (projects, findings, …) use owner_id and policies; the dashboard document store does not mirror that model. If these tables are exposed via PostgREST with default grants, tenant isolation relies entirely on application code and connection string secrecy, not database enforcement.

proof_hooks.value: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS penny_projects ( ... ) — no subsequent ALTER ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY in repo migrations reviewed.
projects table uses owner_id + policies in 20260402163740_rls_policies.sql; penny_* family has no matching pattern.

suggested_fix.approach: Enable RLS on each penny_* table and define policies tied to auth.uid() (or a project membership table), or REVOKE default API role access and restrict to service_role / server-only roles.
steps: Confirm PostgREST exposure and current GRANTs in the live Supabase project.
Add migrations: ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + policies, or REVOKE FROM anon/authenticated as appropriate.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from data id field.

findings.title: penny_finding_status enum includes value not present in TS FindingStatus
findings.description: PostgreSQL enum penny_finding_status includes 'assigned' (20260402163739_lyra_schema.sql). apps/dashboard/lib/types.ts FindingStatus union does not include 'assigned', so rows or API payloads using that value will not type-check in client code and may break narrowing or switches.

proof_hooks.value: 'open', 'accepted', 'assigned', 'in_progress', ...
FindingStatus = "open" | "accepted" | "in_progress" | ...

suggested_fix.approach: Add 'assigned' to FindingStatus or migrate DB to drop/rename the enum value to align with the canonical contract in audits/agent expectations.
steps: Regenerate or hand-sync shared types from Supabase schema.
Run a one-time UPDATE if the value is unused.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from data id field.

findings.title: model_usage has RLS enabled but no policy definitions in migrations
findings.description: 20260402163740_rls_policies.sql enables ROW LEVEL SECURITY on model_usage but does not add any CREATE POLICY. Rows are therefore denied for roles subject to RLS without a bypass. Server code using SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY bypasses RLS (see apps/dashboard/lib/supabase.ts), so ingestion may work, but authenticated or anon clients cannot read model_usage without additional policies—by design or oversight.

proof_hooks.value: ALTER TABLE model_usage ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; — file ends without model_usage policies.
getModelUsage(client) uses .from('model_usage')

suggested_fix.approach: If user-scoped dashboards need cost data under JWT, add SELECT policies joining audit_runs → projects. If only service_role reads this table, document and optionally REVOKE direct API access.
steps: Decide access model for model_usage.
Add policies or document service-only access.

findings.title: repair_service migration DROP POLICY names may not match earlier migration
findings.description: 20260402231116_repair_service.sql drops policies named users_can_view_repair_jobs, etc. The earlier 20260402163740_rls_policies.sql creates policies named "Users can access repair jobs for their projects". IF EXISTS avoids errors, but the older FOR ALL policy may remain alongside newer SELECT/INSERT policies, producing redundant or confusing policy sets until manually reconciled.

proof_hooks.summary: DROP uses different names than CREATE in 002
proof_hooks.value: DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "users_can_view_repair_jobs" vs CREATE POLICY "Users can access repair jobs for their projects"

suggested_fix.approach: In a follow-up migration, list policies on repair_jobs in production and drop obsolete ones explicitly.
steps: SELECT polname FROM pg_policy JOIN pg_class ... WHERE relname = 'repair_jobs';
DROP redundant policies; document intended final set.

proof_hooks.value: GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE ON repair_jobs TO anon, authenticated;

suggested_fix.approach: If only Edge Functions and server should mutate repair_jobs, prefer narrower GRANTs plus strict RLS tests.

Add automated policy tests or integration tests for anon/authenticated.
